name: human-approval
description: Pause workflow execution and require manual human approval before proceeding. Emits an approval request event that must be acknowledged via the API.

REASON.description: Human-readable explanation for why approval is required

APPROVERS.description: Optional comma-separated list of approver identifiers (email addresses or usernames)

setup_instructions.title: Actor capability ceiling — required before adding this node
setup_instructions.detail: The workflow owning actor MUST have max_capability_world: "governance-node". If you see a capability ceiling violation at trigger time, run: grant_capability_ceiling(actor_id: "<actor_id>", world: "governance-node"). Without this, the workflow will fail at execution time with a capability ceiling error even if it builds successfully. Check the actor's current ceiling with get_actor_details.

setup_instructions.title: Configure approval reason
setup_instructions.detail: Set REASON to a clear explanation of what the approver is authorizing. This text will be shown in the approval request. You may use {{field_name}} to interpolate upstream output values — e.g. REASON: "Review payment of {{amount}} to {{vendor}}".

setup_instructions.title: Approve or reject via MCP tool
setup_instructions.detail: When execution pauses at this node, call list_pending_approvals to see waiting approvals, then submit_workflow_approval(execution_id: "<id>", approved: true|false, reason: "...") to unblock the execution. Approved workflows continue from the next node; rejected workflows fail with an 'approval denied' error message.
